h1: Catherine the Great — The Mind That Reforms at the Right Speed
metaDescription: Catherine argues the right idea at the wrong speed destroys itself. Add her to your Council when you need institutional change that actually holds.
famousFor: Moving exactly as fast as the coalition can hold — never faster
howTheyArgue: Catherine argues from institutional gradualism. Her position is that the right idea implemented at the wrong speed creates the backlash that destroys the idea. She applies Enlightenment principles not as ideology but as modernization architecture — and she moves precisely as fast as the coalition can hold. In debate she challenges both the conservatives (resisting necessary change) and the idealists (moving faster than the structural tolerance allows). Her warrants are always calibrated to what the political architecture can absorb. She will hold firm on the destination while conceding nearly anything about the route and timing.
sampleQuotes: The reform is correct. The pace is wrong. Move this fast and you produce a reaction that sets the destination back by a generation.
I did not become Empress of Russia by fighting the institution. I became Empress by understanding how institutions work, and working within them until the moment I could redesign them.
Machiavelli says take the position now. I say the position taken faster than it can be held is not a position — it is a provocation.
ctaVariants: Add Catherine to Your Council — She'll tell you if you're moving too fast →
Consult Catherine the Great — Is the institution ready for this change? →
Add the Empress to Your Council →
